At this point, it is recommended that the server is restarted. ShipExec Configuration Tool Open SE Config Tool by locating the ShipExec Configuration Tool on the desktop and Run as Administrator. It is highly recommended to make a shortcut of this executable and set it to run as an administrator. (1) Load License There are three components needed to install a valid license: ● License ID ● Password ● License file. When a license is installed, it will enable the entitlements for the license. Entitlements will include: ● Core API ● Clients ● Adapters. A valid license is required to start the ShipExecWcfService.exe service. When loading a different license, the ShipExecWcfService.exe service will need to be restarted. 1. Select Licensing from the left pane. 2. Enter License Id and Password, and click Load License 3. In the Open dialog box, select the license text file and

license is required to start the ShipExecWcfService.exe service. When loading a different license, the ShipExecWcfService.exe service will need to be restarted. 1. Select Licensing from the left pane. 2. Enter License Id and Password, and click Load License 3. In the Open dialog box, select the license text file and then click Open. 4. Once the license gets validated, a Success popup appears. Click OK to return to the license page. 5. If an Error popup is displayed, click OK and check the license information and then re-enter the license information. (2) Set connection strings 1. Select AppDb from the left pane a. Enter the connection string b. Click Test button, Save button, Verify button and Migrate button in this respective order NOTE: Connection string sample: server=<ServerName>;database=SE_AppDb;user id=<UserId>;password=<Password>; If the Save button is not enabled after the Test button, click somewhere else in the screen to shift the focus out of the Test button and enable the Save button. 2. Select SoxDb from the left pane a. Enter the connection string b. Click Test button, Save button, Verify button and Migrate button in this respective order NOTE: Connection string sample: server=<ServerName>;database=SE_SoxDb;user id=<UserId>;password=<Password>; If the Save button is not enabled after the Test button, click somewhere else in the screen to shift the focus out of Test button and make the Save button enabled 3. Select Configuration from the left pane a. Click on Update Adapters b. Click on Update ShipExec Info (3) IIS Certification setup 1. Open IIS Manager 2. Copy the names of the ApplicationPools for the API website. By default, they should be: a. ShipExecManagementStudioApiAppPool b. ShipExecThinClientApiAppPool 3. Give the App Pools access to the certificate a. Open a command prompt and enter “mmc” no quotes b. File -> Add/Remove SnapIn -> Highlight Certificates -> Click Add -> Select Computer account -> click Ok c. Expand the Certificates on the left d. Expand Personal or Web Hosting e. Click on the Certificates folder f. Right-click on the certificate being used g. Select All Tasks h. Select Manage Private Keys i. Add the following users i. IIS AppPool\ShipExecManagementStudioApiAppPool ii. IIS AppPool\ShipExecThinClientApiAppPool 4. Restart the workstation or server (not needed but highly recommended) (4) Core Windows Service 1. To confirm that the installation is successful, open Services in Windows. 2. Scroll down and find the service named Shipexec WCF Service. 3. Make sure that the service is running and the Startup type is set to Automatic. Shipexec Management Studio Locate ShipExec Management Studio 2.3 on the desktop and open it. (1) Create Admin user on first login 1. When you launch Management Studio for the first time, it will prompt you to create an administrator account in order to login. This account is the primary administrative account. You may later create multiple admin accounts, but this is the first admin account permitted in the system. 2. Enter email address, password, confirm password, and click on Create. 3. The administrator account will be created and the user will navigate to the

This account is the primary administrative account. You may later create multiple admin accounts, but this is the first admin account permitted in the system. 2. Enter email address, password, confirm password, and click on Create. 3. The administrator account will be created and the user will navigate to the Management Studio Welcome page. (2) Add Company 1. Click on the Company menu to navigate to the Company configuration page. A company is used to relate shippers, adapters, printers, document types, etc. 2. Click on the Add Company button. 3. The Add Company popup screen will appear for the user. 4. Enter: a. Company Name b. Symbol c. License ID. License Id is a mandatory field to add a company. 5. Click on Save button to save the details. 6. The new company will be added to the list. (3) Shippers 1. Click on the button at the right hand side of the company to edit it. Editing a company means you can add shippers, adapters printer configurations, and any other configurations for the company. 2. Click on Shippers menu Note: In 2.x for every shipper there should be an entry here. If there is more than one shipper in Connectship all of them have to be added here. 3. Click on the Add Shipper button to go to the Add Shipper popup 4. Enter ● Shipper name ● Symbol ● Company Name ● Contact, Address ● Phone ● Email 5. Click Save (4) Add Adapters 1. Click on Adapters menu 2. Click on the Add Adapters 3. Select Adapter from the list and Save. (5) Add Shipper/Adapter Mappings 1. Click on the Edit button to add shipper mapping to the adapter. 2. Go to the Mappings tab and select the Shipexec Shipper and Adapter Shipper and click on the Add Shipper Mapping 3. Save the mapping 4. Click on the down arrow button on the right hand side of the adapter for running the maintenance. 5. After the maintenance is done, the status will be updated as Ready and Adapter information Updated Successfully message will be displayed at the bottom (6) Printer Definition NOTE: You can get the details for the printer configuration from the Printing section of the 1.X Management Studio. 1. Navigate to the Printer Definition page by selecting the menu Printing->Printer Definitions 2. Click on Add Printer Definition. 3. Enter ○ Printer Alias ○ Printer Model ○ Printer Stock ○ Printer Direction ○ Action to Perform ○ Image Type if the Action to Perform is “Image”. 4. Click on Save. (7) Document Configuration 1. Navigate to the Document Configuration page by selecting the menu Printers->Document Configurations. 2. Click on Add Document Configuration to create a document profile 3. Enter profile name and Save. 4. Click on the edit button to add Document Configuration details. 5. Click on the Add Document button to add the details . 6. Select ○ Carrier ○ Printer Alias ○ Document Format 7. Click Save. 8. Save all documents added using the

profile 3. Enter profile name and Save. 4. Click on the edit button to add Document Configuration details. 5. Click on the Add Document button to add the details . 6. Select ○ Carrier ○ Printer Alias ○ Document Format 7. Click Save. 8. Save all documents added using the Save button. 9. Click on the edit button any time to update the document details. (8) Printer Configurations 1. Navigate to the Printer configuration page by selecting the menu Printers->Printer Configurations. 2. Click on Add Printer Configuration to create a Printer profile name. This can be repeated for each shipper. 3. Add Name and Save. 4. Click on the Edit button to add Printer configuration details. 5. Click on the button on the Available printer list to move it to the selected list and Save. 6. You can remove the selected printer using the Remove button at the selected printer list if it is not required. 7. Update Printer Configuration Successfully message will be displayed at the bottom of the page. (9) Add Default Profile Profiles allow you to configure the user experience within the ShipExec Thin Client or hardware and document settings (among many other things) for ShipExec Management Studio or the ShipExec Web Service. 1. On the ShipExec Management Studio home page, click Companies. 2. From the Companies page, to add a profile at the company level, click the icon for the company to which you want to add a profile. 3. Click the Profiles menu. 4. Click on the Add Profile button 5. Type the name of the profile and click Save. The saved profile displays. (10) Edit Default Profile 1. Click the icon to see the details. 2. Add the description in the General tab 3. Click the Shippers tab. 4. In the Available Shippers panel, click the shipper you want to add to this profile, then click the icon to move the shipper to the Selected Shippers panel. 5. Click the Carriers tab. 6. In the Available Carriers panel, select the carrier(s) you want to add to this profile, then click the icon to move the carriers to the Selected Carriers panel. 1. Click the Services tab. 2. In the Available Services panel, select the service(s) you want to add to this profile, then click the icon to move the service to the Selected Services panel. Note: The Services you move to the right panel are the ones that will appear in the Thin Client or custom Shipping software or SAP application. 3. Click the Countries tab. 4. In the Available Countries panel, select the countries you want to add to this profile, then click the icon to move the countries to the Selected Countries panel. 5. Click the Package Types tab. 6. In the Available Package Types panel, select the package type(s) you want to add to this profile, then click the icon to move the package types to the Selected Package Types panel. Note: Use the up and down arrows to rearrange the

panel. 5. Click the Package Types tab. 6. In the Available Package Types panel, select the package type(s) you want to add to this profile, then click the icon to move the package types to the Selected Package Types panel. Note: Use the up and down arrows to rearrange the package types hierarchy. Click the icon to update the package type with a custom name. 7. Click the Payment Term tab. 8. In the Available Payment Terms panel, select the payment term(s) you want to add to this profile, then click the icon to move the payment terms to the Selected Payment Terms panel. Note: Use the up and down arrows to rearrange the payment terms hierarchy. Click the icon to update the payment terms with a custom name. 9. Click the Hardware Configuration tab. 10. From the Document Configuration drop-down list, select the document configuration you want to assign to this profile. From the Printer Configuration drop-down list, select the printer configuration you want to assign to this profile.

Sparq Internal Use Only ShipExec / ConnectShip installation and configuration Note: All steps below should be followed in order, and if a section is unable to be completed, please do not continue and get assistance from the team. This is especially important on a customer’s server. ConnectShip credentials ● (If you are installing on a customer’s server and upgrading from an older ShipExec 1.x to 2.x, they will already have ConnectShip credentials, which you’ll need to request. For your own local install, use the steps below.) ● You will need to to register with ConnectShip to receive a custom login, and be included on the UPS License and access its Carriers, Services, Printers and other settings. This is important for installing ConnectShip later on in this guide. ● Use this ConnectShip URl to register for Member Access, to have your credentials created: https://connectship.com/contact/?ref=register. During registration, on the line for the ConnectShip License ID, use CH89725. That ID was created for Sparq and our local setups only. ● Usually the next day, ConnectShip will email you your new user ID and initial password. Go to the main ConnectShip home, then to the Member home page and log in with the temporary credentials, then change your password ● Review the License and Password information on the right hand side of the page. Show the password and copy the credentials. That will be the license and password used in the ConnectShip PMC licensing configuration later on. Install Chrome ● Download and install Chrome if it has not been already. ● UPS highly recommends Chrome to be installed and used with ShipExec 2, but MS Edge is also acceptable if a customer requires using it instead. Install .Net Framework 4.8 ● .Net 4.8 is required for ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip, and might already be installed depending on the windows or server version. You can confirm the version installed by these steps: ○ Run regedit.exe, either from the Windows > Run box or a command prompt. ○ Open the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Microsoft\NET Framework Setup\NDP\v4\Full folder. ○ Note the Version value in the right pane. If it’s below 4.8, download and install that version in the following steps: ■ Download and install and take defaults during the setup: https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet-framework/net48 ■ Note: For installing on a customer’s server, select the .Net Framework 4.8 Runtime. If installing for your local setup, select the Developer Pack. Install IIS and other features ● For a local install on Windows 10, open Control Panel then Programs and Features. On the left select Turn Windows features on or off. Make sure at least the following are selected, and use the images below as a full guide. Note the gray checkmarks aren’t disabled items, it’s just the current view. Those items should be installed. ● For installing on a customer’s server, open the Server Roles and Features. Sparq internal use only Below are screenshots for the minimum Roles and Features required for ShipExec 2.x, found on Windows Server 2012 and newer, to use as a
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx (1).pdf

Text: just the current view. Those items should be installed. ● For installing on a customer’s server, open the Server Roles and Features. Sparq internal use only Below are screenshots for the minimum Roles and Features required for ShipExec 2.x, found on Windows Server 2012 and newer, to use as a guide. Similar selections exist for a local setup on Windows 10. You can select more options if needed for your particular setup. (The screenshots may indicate .Net 4.7, even if 4.8 is installed) Note: If installing ShipExec 2.x on a customer’s server where an older ShipExec already exists, leave any current selections checked to support the older product. To avoid errors with the server being unable to locate source media to install features, it’s recommended to select features under the Web Server section, install them, then open the wizard again to add other features below it and restart as needed. Sparq internal use only Notes: ● ASP is legacy but still required to install ConnectShip (PMC), if installing on a new server. ● IIS 6 Management Compatibility isn’t required for ShipExec 2.x and can be unchecked, only if installing on a new server. If the server has an existing ShipExec 1.x setup, leave the option checked. Sparq internal use only Install SQL and Tools ● (If installing on a customer’s server and they already have SQL Server, skip this step) ● For a local setup, you can install MS SQL Express Server 2019. (Or you can download SQL Developer edition for a SQL server with less limitations and more options): ○ https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=101064 ● If installing on a customer’s server, SQL Express will not be sufficient - the 10 GB max database size limit will cause issues with PMC/ConnectShip. The customer will need to provide a SQL server instance to use that fits their business requirements. ● Install SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS). This is not required but very convenient for working either locally or on a customer’s shipexec server to access the DB, instead of having to remote into their external SQL server. ○ You can typically install the latest version of SSMS. ○ Note: If installing on older Server 2012, it’s recommended to download an older version of SSMS such as 2016, since newer versions install Azure Data services which is a large part of the install and unnecessary for ShipExec. Older SSMS versions can be found on the download site under Previous Versions. ○ https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/ssms/download-sql-server-management-studio-ssms?view =sql-server-ver15 Configure SQL Databases ● Open SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) ● Click Connect ○ For a local setup, log into SQL using default Windows Authentication for now. ○ If installing on a customer’s server, log into SQL using a predefined SQL user they should have provided, which needs either dbcreator server role or db_owner database role, or similar. The login needs full read/write/create/delete permissions to create databases and modify table data. ● Right click on the SQL server node itself, then under Properties > Security ○ Make sure SQL Server and Windows
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx (1).pdf

Text: they should have provided, which needs either dbcreator server role or db_owner database role, or similar. The login needs full read/write/create/delete permissions to create databases and modify table data. ● Right click on the SQL server node itself, then under Properties > Security ○ Make sure SQL Server and Windows Authentication mode is checked ● If installing for a local setup: ○ Build a progistics user - “progistics” username is common for many installations ■ Under Security > Logins > right click then New Login... ■ For the default local install you can just use: Sparq internal use only ● Username: progistics, password: progistics (ok for local, not for customer's server) ■ Uncheck Enforce password expiration ■ On Server Roles page check sysadmin (ok for local) ● Log out of SSMS and back in using your new progistics account. It should allow you to connect and access the Databases. (On a customer server, verify the account provided allows you to connect to the server.) ● Create these 5 DBs as named below (these are not added or predefined by the ConnectShip software which is installed later). All databases here are needed for a local setup. ● If installing on a customer server and the customer has an older ShipExec version, you will still need to create custom named databases for 2.x. ○ You will need to run a specified script after creating the first 3 databases below. (If the scripts aren’t available in a zip file in the Installation folder, you’ll need to copy them from the ConnectShip “Database Use and Distributed Installation” page: https://connectship.com/docs/A00028.aspx. Click on each SQL Server Transact-SQL Script link then copy the SQL script as needed.) ○ CS_Controller ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Controller). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Controller name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with the environment tag (_Dev) to differentiate it, such as CS_Controller2.x_Dev. ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ■ Select the DB, select New Query, then run the Controller Data Storage script (dbcontroller_60.sql) ○ CS_Transactions ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Trans). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Trans name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with the environment tag (_Dev) to differentiate it such as CS_Trans2.x_Dev. ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ■ Select the DB, select New Query, then run the Transaction Data Storage script (default_trans_62.sql) ○ CS_Configuration ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Config). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Config name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx (1).pdf

Text: ○ CS_Configuration ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Config). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Config name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with the environment tag (_Dev) to differentiate it such as CS_Config2.x_Dev. ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ■ Select the DB, select New Query, then run the Server Component Configuration Data Storage (dbconfig_65.sql) ■ If this is a local install using only SQL Express: ● For this DB only, on the Files settings, on the LOG row edit the Autogrowth / Maxsize. Set Maximum File Size to 10,000 MB. ● On the Options settings set Auto Shrink to true ○ SE_AppDb ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ○ SE_SoxDb ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) Sparq internal use only Install UPS Specific Software Install ConnectShip and PMC ConnectShip and Progistics Management Console (PMC) are used to configure shippers and carriers used by ShipExec. PMC is installed along with ConnectShip, and is an integral part of ShipExec. It communicates with carriers during shipping and generates tracking numbers and labels. This section is only needed for a local install, or on a new customer's server. If a customer's server already has an older ShipExec version and PMC already installed, you can skip this section. ● For a customer install: ○ Connect to their 1.x Prod server and open PMC as an admin. Click Server Components and note or take a screenshot of the components listed to use in the new server setup. ● Log into the ConnectShip Members Area page then under Members, select Downloads. Click the ConnectShip Toolkit link, then on that page download the ConnectShip Toolkit 6.5 (not the Client-only), and install as an administrator. ● During the install, on the selection page under Server Components: ○ For a local setup, select just UPS (Consolidated) for now. ○ If it’s for a customer server, refer back to the Prod PMC server components notes or screenshot earlier and select those components in the list. This will make importing their PMC config later on less problematic. (Common customer server carriers might include Endicia, Global, LTL, UPS Freight, UPS (Consolidated), USPS (Endicia), etc.) ● Under Device Components, for Printers select PCL, Postscript, Windows, Zebra/Eltron ● When prompted to enter a Progistics user account password, if it’s for a local setup you can try progistics, but depending on the system you may need a simple but strong password, and be sure to note it ● When asked any questions regarding installing or updating SQL, always select NO ● If prompted to stop IIS during the install, select YES ● When prompted to download any available updates, select NO. Updates can be applied after install. ○ Note: Waiting on upgrading is also very important if you’re going to be connecting PMC to an existing
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx (1).pdf

Text: SQL, always select NO ● If prompted to stop IIS during the install, select YES ● When prompted to download any available updates, select NO. Updates can be applied after install. ○ Note: Waiting on upgrading is also very important if you’re going to be connecting PMC to an existing 1.x PMC version (more advanced setup), if a customer has 1.x installed. If you upgrade PMC it could be out of sync with their current 1.x version. ● When prompted to run maintenance, select NO. There will be post-installation Maintenance errors, select OK and ignore any that display. You will be installing updates at a later step once PMC is installed and configured. ● Once PMC is installed, open it as admin under Start > ConnectShip Progistics, and run the PMC (not the 32-bit version). Be sure to right click and run as an admin or you won’t be able to configure things. ● Note: It’s advised to change the start menu shortcut to always run PMC as admin under the properties. If PMC is not run as an admin, most options will be disabled and grayed out. Configure PMC Connections If a customer has an older ShipExec version already installed on the server, it’s recommended to use their existing ConnectShip Progistics Management Console (PMC) config, and you can skip this section. ● If you need to install PMC on a new customer’s server, access their current 1.x Dev server and export their current config with these steps: ○ Connect to their Dev server and run PMC as administrator Sparq internal use only ○ Enterprise > Import/Export Settings Data ○ Export Settings Data section, click Browse and select a path for the xml file ○ Export ○ Copy the xml file to the new Dev server, to be used later ● Open PMC as an administrator ● Load the license in PMC ○ Enterprise > Licensing ■ If you don’t already have the ConnectShip license from earlier steps, go to the ConnectShip site and when logged in, on the Members Home page, at the top there should be a License ID dropdown that lists all customer licenses you have access to. ■ For a local setup, use the license ID in the dropdown of CH89725, and if it’s not displayed, search for it in the dropdown. (That ID is general purpose assigned to Sparq only for our local development and testing, but not for customer use.) Copy the License Code and Password for use later. ■ If you’re installing PMC on a new customer’s server, look for the customer’s company name in the License dropdown in the top right. Securing ShipExec in IIS ● For a local setup, the default access for running ShipExec’s Thin Client, Management Studio and Web Service is via http. If you choose to use https to mimic how it might work on a customer's server, use the following steps. Otherwise you can skip this section. ● The ShipExecClientSupport installs a ShipExecClientService cert in Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager. That certificate is used by the server to communicate with the Client Support tool to access workstation machines and devices. You do not need to enable https for the Client Support tool to work. ● Note: On a customer’s server, using the ShipExecClientService cert for https is not allowed, per UPS. The customer will need to provide and install their own server SSL cert in IIS to be used with ShipExec. The type and strength of the cert is up to the customer and their organization’s IT standards. ● For a local setup, or if on a customer’s server and a custom SSL cert is installed, you can enable https with bindings and Require SSL settings. ● For initial testing of a local setup (or even on a customer’s server), you can leave the https settings unchecked only for testing the Thin Client and Management Studio. ● To enable https, open IIS and select the Default Web Site ○ Click Bindings… on the right ○ In the Site Bindings page click Add… Sparq internal use only ○ In the Type: dropdown select https, then in the SSL certificate dropdown select ShipExecClientService (only for your local install), or select the customer’s certificate, then OK ● Select ShipExecManagementStudio, then open SSL Settings ○ Check Require SSL and Client certificates: Accept, then Apply on the right ● For ShipExecThinClient, set Require SSL and Client certificates: Accept, then Apply on the right Confirm ShipExec is working ● Open ShipExec Management Studio on the desktop and confirm you get a login page. ○ If prompted with a private security warning, select Advanced then proceed ○ You will be prompted to create a ShipExec Administrator login, which is the “super admin” that’s for all companies on that server, used to add other users, and configure the company in Management Studio. Those steps are performed in the next section. ○ Create an admin-type named user with a secure password. You will use this admin account to log into Management Studio and manage all company config going forward. It should not be provided to the customer, so that our team has a login to connect and manage their company config. You will create a customer admin login in the next section. ShipExec company setup ShipExec Management Studio is an admin portal that allows you to create a company, manage customer company configurations, Thin Client settings, and add/modify users for the company. Before using the Thin Client, you will need to add a new company (you won’t need to add an Enterprise or Site, just a Company). ● Add Company ○ The Name is the company name, and typically matches what is used in PMC for the customer (or your local test company) under a Shipper Company name ○ The Symbol is a short and unique version of the company name, in all caps ○ The License ID should be the ShipExec license number, entered in the ShipExec config to load the license (usually 5 digits) ● Edit the company to continue ● For a local setup: ○ Add a Shipper under Shippers. You only need the Name, Symbol and Company for now, and it should be like the shipper you added in PMC in earlier steps. Open PMC as admin if needed to find a shipper. ● For a customer’s server ○ You will need to add the Name, Symbol (abbreviation), and Company for each Shipper in PMC. Text: ShipExec Closeout Tool Instructions This guide will go over steps to set up an automated closeout tool for ShipExec 2.x that works similarly to the 1.x closeout tool. All of these files/folders need to be placed somewhere that can be accessed easily. Then the ShipExecEOD.exe.config needs to be updated. There are 4 values that need to be updated. 1. [Server Path] needs to be replaced with the base URL for the ShipExec Web Service. That can be found by opening the Web Service in IIS and copying the value 2. [Access Client Key] The key for the Client that was created in Management Studio for API interaction. 3. [Access Client Name] The name of the Client that was created in Management Studio for API interaction. 4. [Company Id] The GUID for the Company - can be found on the General page for the Company. Additionally, NLog.config can be configured to change logging information (follow standard NLog conventions). Inside this folder, a bat file also needs to be created. Inside this file, a line needs to be added for every Shipper/Service combination that needs to be closed out. The format for those lines is ShipExecEOD <SHIPPER> <CARRIER> The file should be in the same directory as ShipExecEOD.exe. For example: After the files are set up, a scheduled task needs to be created. First open Windows Task Scheduler. Then create a new folder After that, right click on the new folder and select Create Basic Task Give the task a name and description Then select when the task should start Fine tune when the task should run On the next screen, select Start a Program. Then browse to and add the .bat file to the command line

If a certificate is not available, one option is to create a Self-Signed Certificate in IIS to be used for ShipExec. Please use the following steps to create a self-signed certificate. Open IIS Manager and select the server Click on Server Certificates Click on Create Self-Signed Certificate Give the certificate a name (“ShipExec2Cert” for example) and Select Web Hosting for the certificate store. Once the certificate is created, click on Default Web Site Then click on Bindings.. on the right hand side Add a site binding for https. Enter the server name or address in the Host name field and select the certificate. Then click Ok.

ShipExec Overview Welcome to the wonderful world of ShipExec! Let’s begin by going over what ShipExec actually is. Basically, it is a platform that acts as an interface to another application - that application is ConnectShip which is used for shipping information. An example of the use of ShipExec might be that Company ABC sells and ships products to customers. When they have enough orders, they want to get a rate of how much it will cost to ship those products. To do so, they use ShipExec. The shipping information (to address, from address, weight, packaging, etc.) is used by ShipExec to create API calls into ConnectShip. Rates are returned through ShipExec. Many things can happen in ShipExec to further massage the data (for example, applying a discount for a certain product type). These rates are then returned to the customer so they can decide which method to use to Ship (multiple rates are returned). ShipExec is a huge platform made up of many products that work together. It is natural to feel a bit overwhelmed while reading this guide. Learning ShipExec will be a process and although this guide will cover a lot of information, no one document can capture everything. Getting Started Here are some basic steps to getting started with ShipExec and the ShipExec team: Replace existing Sparq laptop with a beefier development machine Please note that even on the better development laptop you will still not have administrator access - most of the things you will be doing with ShipExec will be in a VM where you do have administrator privileges Access existing VMs ( ) ShipExec VM Exports provided a big boost to the team by creating shared VMs Jeremy Swineheart You should have 4 VMs DB ShipExec 1.12 ShipExec 2.2 ShipExec 2.3 Once UPS ID assigned, go to UPSers.com and establish “Challenge Questions” Download Microsoft Authenticator app on phone RMA Token Registration (wait for email from ARPS@ups.com) Download SecurID Authenticator app on phone Register token Setup PIN Log In to UPS Email (This is for Outlook email and not the inside.ups.com link or upsers.com link in the documentation) These are the basic steps. For detailed information, please review the following document: . UPS Team Member Onboarding Resources.docx There are also tasks for onboarding that others will need to do. ConnectionShip - Toolkit is a full-featured, server-based shipping API known industry-wide for its scalability and reliability. ShipExec Core / WCF Service The ShipExec Core is the supporting structure. The Core is the traffic manager, translator, and data manager of the ShipExec system. Everything that happens in ShipExec flows through the WCF Service. ShipExec Management Studio The ShipExec Management Studio is the command center for ShipExec. The Management Studio is where you can customize and configure ShipExec to meet your specific needs. This is used to configure ShipExec so that it can be used to rate/ship/etc. Standard users will not be able to view or change things here. Likewise, this user cannot use it to ship or rate. It is only used for configuration. ShipExec Thin Client The ShipExec Clients like the Thin Client provide a modern user interface and while maintaining our classic layout. Our ShipExec clients give you access to a full-featured shipping experience. This is the product that users think of when they hear ShipExec. It allows them to input shipping information and then make decisions based on the result. For example, a user could input shipping info (address to, weight, packaging, etc.) then rate against services to see the different prices for shipping. ShipExec Web Service The ShipExec Web Service is a developer client that enables you to access shipping services you need for your business. Terminology Many, many different terms in ShipExec may seem foreign at first. Some basics: Shipper A ShipExec Shipper defines locations for shipping. A one shipper supports your list of carriers with a single carrier account. ShipExec Overview Welcome to the wonderful world of ShipExec! Let’s begin by going over what ShipExec actually is. Basically, it is a platform that acts as an interface to another application - that application is ConnectShip which is used for shipping information. An example of the use of ShipExec might be that Company ABC sells and ships products to customers. When they have enough orders, they want to get a rate of how much it will cost to ship those products. To do so, they use ShipExec. The shipping information (to address, from address, weight, packaging, etc.) is used by ShipExec to create API calls into ConnectShip. Rates are returned through ShipExec. Many things can happen in ShipExec to further massage the data (for example, applying a discount for a certain product type). These rates are then returned to the customer so they can decide which method to use to Ship (multiple rates are returned). ShipExec is a huge platform made up of many products that work together. It is natural to feel a bit overwhelmed while reading this guide. Learning ShipExec will be a process and although this guide will cover a lot of information, no one document can capture everything. How to do rate shopping? ● The weight of the load is required for rate shopping. ● If PackageDefaults.Weight is null, use the configured weight. ● A service list is required for rate shopping. If the service list is empty, no rates will be returned. ● Use businessObject.Rate with the following parameters: GetUserContext, ShipmentRequest, ServiceList, SortType, and UserParameters. ● From the response, get the cheapest rate and return it to the frontend. ● Set DefaultPackages.Service with the selected return type. How to access Atlas locally? Atlas is the Azure environment used for setting up custom shipping: http://svrt00010cfe.tus.ams1907.com/Customers/Viewserve/655?custId=1433 ● When adding the adapter, use the Atlas URL in both the AMP URL and AMP Admin URL. This maps the shipper's setup to the cloud. ● Browse the Atlas URL above using your username and password to log in. ● Use RSI Dev for configuration, and RSI Shipper for testing and training. ● Click on the main company, download the toolkit, and export/import settings. ● After downloading the toolkit, you’ll find three zip files. ● Unzip all folders, copy the config file, and drop it into the TKCToolsAndLibrary folder. ● Use PMC.exe in the TKCToolsAndLibrary folder to configure. Tips for converting from 1.x to 2.x: ● If any code or variables are unnecessary, do not convert them. ● Replace SoxDictionaryItem with SerializableDictionary. ● The Business_Rules class contains reusable methods. Use the BusinessObjectAPI object instead of ManagementLayer. What is the difference between 1.x and 2.x web services? ● By default, RESTful endpoints in 2.x use JSON for complex datasets. ● SOAP-based services use XML. ● Authentication now requires a Client ID and Access Key, which are randomly generated. ● Access keys can be generated at the system, company, or site level. How to import a company from Dev to UAT? ● Before deleting in DEV, go to company search and export the company (for rollback purposes). ● Click on the Import Company button to import it into UAT. ● If needed, update the symbol in the XML before import. ● After import, update the adapter URL for UAT/Production. ● If the project is moving to Production and SBR needs to be deployed, you do not need to import the company. Just raise a helpdesk ticket. Installation of 2.3 version details ● Two new installers: 1. Shipexec Management Studio API Setup 2. Shipexec Thin Client API Setup ● APIs can be installed on the same server or a different one. ● The Management Studio (MS) communicates with the API, which in turn communicates with Core. ● HTTPS is now mandatory in version 2.3. ● Same server: use NetNamedPipes. Different server: use NetTCP or HTTP. (Note: NetTCP is faster than HTTP, though HTTP is only slightly slower.) ● The API looks for certificates. Retrieve the thumbprint from the Certificate Manager for the local system. o Default certificate: ShipExecClientService (self-signed, created during installation). ● If a new self-signed certificate is used, update the thumbprint and URLs in the Management Studio API Config file. ● Update the

slightly slower.) ● The API looks for certificates. Retrieve the thumbprint from the Certificate Manager for the local system. o Default certificate: ShipExecClientService (self-signed, created during installation). ● If a new self-signed certificate is used, update the thumbprint and URLs in the Management Studio API Config file. ● Update the thin client config.json file with the correct API URL to ensure correct communication in SBR/CBR. ● Ensure Shipexec Client Service is running to use client support services. ● App pools must have full rights to the certificate in the personal folder in MMC. (e.g., user/group: App pool\<name of the App pool from IIS>) How to add a ticket to the helpdesk for moving SBR? 1. Go to Shipexec Support. 2. Enter the contact's name. 3. Enter the company name. 4. Enter the subject (e.g., Hosting request: Upload SBR to Production). 5. Enter the severity level (with justification). 6. Add a description (e.g., Please upload the newest SBR to Atlas Prod with the company name and number). 7. Set the Request Type: non-support-related or internal UPS ticket. 8. Topic: Hosting request. 9. Specify the version. 10. Attach the SBR DLL file. 11. Submit the ticket. 12. Close the ticket once completed. How to handle biological substances or commodities in Restricted Article shipping requests? ● RestrictedArticleType is a field only available in ConnectShip. ● There is no direct field for this in Shipexec shipping requests. ● Use the PackageExtra object in ShipmentPackage to pass this value as a SerializableDictionary: Package.PackageExtra = new SerializableDictionary { ("RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE", 32) }; ● 32 indicates a commodity with biological substances (e.g., blood samples). ● The label will display "ISC-BIO" in the bottom right-hand corner. 1.X Communications Setup ● In Management Studio 1.x: Go to Communication → WCF Instances (create WCF instance if it’s the first time). ● Ensure the instance is running to enable Business Rules. How do you turn on the 1.X Business Rules hooks? Go to Configuration → AppDb → Business Rules, and enable the hooks. How to add global variables in 1.X? 1. Open the Database menu on the right-hand side. 2. Create a new global variable. 3. Set the Database Name as the variable name. 4. Set the Database Type to Custom. 5. Enter the variable value in the Database Connection String field.

1. Source Control Guide Prerequisites ● Box.com Account through UPS ● Box.com Drive (https://www.box.com/resources/downloads) ● Administrator rights on the UPS laptop (for Box.com) ● Visit this link on a UPS laptop, if you do not have permissions, request them ○ https://upsinc.sharepoint.com/sites/o365-SOLDIShippingSolutionsTeam/Custome r%20Document%20Management%20Library/Forms/AllItems.aspx?viewpath=%2 Fsites%2Fo365-SOLDIShippingSolutionsTeam%2FCustomer%20Document%20 Management%20Library%2FForms%2FAllItems.aspx&id=%2Fsites%2Fo365-SO LDIShippingSolutionsTeam%2FCustomer%20Document%20Management%20Li brary%2FCustomers&viewid=89107caa-acac-4e1e-bc4c-63b4272bf46f On UPS machine: 1. Start by creating or logging into your UPS Box.com account a. Going to Box.com while on a UPS laptop will auto direct you to the UPS page 2. Download Box.com Drive using the link the above a. Log in with your UPS account b. In Windows Explorer, you should see a new section for Box.com c. Create a new folder “Projects” d. Add a folder under that for each client project 3. Repeat step 2 for your Blackbox laptop. 4. Repeat step 2 on any client machine where you will need to access the code. 5. The structure for uploading files will be using the standard SBR template and adding a CBR folder then putting the CBR javascript in that folder. 6. Add all necessary files from to the Box.com drive to be able to push those files up into Source Control. 7. Once on the UPS Laptop, move the files from Box.com drive to a different folder prior to trying to push to Git - it does not seem to work directly from that drive due to permission issues Depending on the configuration of your UPS Machine, it may have a newer PSI.Sox dll. This might mean that the project will not build on your UPS Machine. If this happens, create a “References” folder in the solution and add a copy of the PSI.Sox dll from the client machine. Expand references, find PSI.Sox, and view properties to get the file path. Setting up a Repo Note that source control should ONLY contain code/projects. Other files will go into Sharepoint, which is detailed later. The “DevOps - Add New Repo” document is well done. The steps that follow are more or less in that document, but they appear here to avoid going through multiple documents. The following steps should be done on your UPS laptop while connected to the UPS VPN. 1. Navigate to tfs.ups.com/tfs/UpsProd/P01Git_CustSol_AST_Integration 2. Click the + button to the right of “P01SGit_CustSol_AST_Integration” and select “New repository” in the dropdown menu. 3. The naming convention for Repos are as follows: “CustomerName - NameOfProduct” a. For Booksource, it is “Booksource - SE2” b. NameOfProduct should be SE1 or SE2 for most of our applications, it’s the version of ShipExec that is being worked on 4. Click the Create button on the lower right of the screen. 5. Copy the URL that is generated for the repo (should be in the address bar). For example: https://tfs.ups.com/tfs/UpsProd/P01SGit_CustSol_AST_Integration/_git/Booksource%20- %20SE2 6. Open the solution in the Box drive in Visual Studio 2019. 7. In the lower right corner, click “Add To Source Control”. 8. Click “Git”. 9. Select the “Existing remote” option. 10. Paste the URL in the Remote URL field. 11. Click

the address bar). For example: https://tfs.ups.com/tfs/UpsProd/P01SGit_CustSol_AST_Integration/_git/Booksource%20- %20SE2 6. Open the solution in the Box drive in Visual Studio 2019. 7. In the lower right corner, click “Add To Source Control”. 8. Click “Git”. 9. Select the “Existing remote” option. 10. Paste the URL in the Remote URL field. 11. Click “Create and Push”. 12. If you see an error about failing to push the branch, click on the up arrow and you should be able to enter your AD information (I had to anyway, without seeing the symbol). 13. Wait until code/branch is pushed. 14. Go back to the project Git page (the copied link). 15. Click the “Create a pull request” button. 16. Enter a title (“First pull for <PROJECT>”) and any other information you want, but leave Reviewers empty (they do not have that option yet). 17. Scroll down and click “Create”. 18. On the next screen, click Approve then Complete. 19. Click “Complete Merge”. 20. Make sure that the files are there by clicking on “Files” under “Repos” on the left navigation pane. 21. Copy all files back to the Box.com drive project location. Non-code Files Sometimes there are files like document types (which should contain both template and control files), rate files, custom DB scripts, etc. that need to be stored. According to UPS, we should not put these items in source control. 1. Visit this link and request access if you do not already have it: a. https://upsinc.sharepoint.com/sites/o365-SOLDIShippingSolutionsTeam/Custome r%20Document%20Management%20Library/Forms/AllItems.aspx?viewpath=%2 Fsites%2Fo365-SOLDIShippingSolutionsTeam%2FCustomer%20Document%20 Management%20Library%2FForms%2FAllItems.aspx&id=%2Fsites%2Fo365-SO LDIShippingSolutionsTeam%2FCustomer%20Document%20Management%20Li brary%2FCustomers&viewid=89107caa-acac-4e1e-bc4c-63b4272bf46f 2. Click the Sync button for this page to create a folder on your machine that will contain these files. 3. For each client, run the file “Add New Project.cmd” a. Enter client name b. Enter product 4. It’s recommended that you organize this folder with project folders for each client. 5. Move the non-code files into the client folder. Client Cleanup 1. Uninstall Box.com Drive from client machines. 2. Make sure the latest code/files are all stored in Git/Customer Document Library.

SSO Setup ● Log into their UAT Management Studio ● On the General tab change the SSO Protocol to SAML 2.0 ● Click on the Thin Client tab under SAML Configuration ● Make sure the Service Provider is ShipExec Thin Client and click on the Download Metadata button Send the Metadata file to the customer. Customer then need to use that to create the ultimate XML SAML file that needs to be uploaded (using the Upload Metadata button in the Identity Provider section). If you are getting the error “Unable to load certificate from store”. Update the thumbprint in MS config file.

Steps for adding a Test printer for DSE on local environment Below are the steps to add a test printer for use in the Direct ShipExec application on our local environment, in order to test the document print / reprint functionality. Step 1: Adding printer on Windows On Windows 11 PC, go to Printers and Scanners -> Add Device Add a new printer named ‘ShipExecPrinter’ (or any other name you would like to call it). Set the driver as ‘Generic / Text Only’. In the Sharing Tab, check the checkbox named 'Share this printer' and set the 'Share name' same as the Printer name and click OK. Step 2: Adding the printer for DSE Now, add this windows printer for use in the DSE project, either by using the CS IRMS Interface WinForms app or directly adding it from SSMS using an insert script If using the IRMS interface app, navigate to 'Printer Stations Setup' and click 'Add Printer'. As the printer we added in Step 1 is marked as shared, if you navigate to '\\Hostname\SharedPrinterName', in my case '\\USREMWFHCSPL2J3\ShipExecPrinter', you should be able to access your printer queue. Add the same using the IRMS interface app: Alternatively, you can add it directly from SSMS: Now your test printer is available for use from the Direct ShipExec application for test print. Make sure the value for PrinterAssigned is true/1. You are good to go!
Source: Steps for adding a Test printer for DSE on local environment.pdf

Introduction The following contains the general terms and conditions of contract under which United Parcel Service, Inc. (“UPS”), the Ohio corporation (certificate number MC-115495) is engaged in the transportation of Package shipments itself and jointly through interchange with its affiliates via the services described below. The UPS Tariff/Terms and Conditions of Service (“Terms”) are effective on the date set forth above and are subject to change without prior notice. The Terms are published electronically on the UPS website (ups.com). The most current and controlling version of the Terms is pub lished at ups.com/termsofservice. In tendering a Shipment for service, the Shipper agrees that the version of the Terms and the applicable UPS Rate and Service Guide (“Service Guide”) in effect at the time of shipping will apply to the Shipment and its transportation (however, the binding arbitration requirement in Section 55 applies to all disputes within its scope, even as to shipments tendered before the effective date of these Terms). Commodities Handled and Restrictions on Service UPS holds itself out to transport general com modities, as usually defined, subject to the following restrictions. In determining the late payment fee for the Monthly Prepayment Plan and the Weekly Prepayment Plan, UPS will apply the prepayment amounts on account with UPS to reduce the calculated base amount on which the late payment fee is assessed, provided that UPS will so apply the prepayment amounts to the oldest outstanding invoice first, and to each subsequent invoice thereafter, until the prepayment amounts have been fully so applied. If an invoice past due is fully so covered by the prepayment amount, no late payment fee will apply. If an invoice past due is partially so covered by the pre- payment amount, the late payment fee will apply to the portion not so covered by the prepayment amount. For the Special Payment Plan, a late payment fee will not be assessed if there has been a positive balance in the Shipper’s prepayment account with UPS at any time during the 27-day period immediately preceding the transmittal of the current invoice to the Shipper. Arbitrate Claims Claimant and UPS agree that, except for disputes that qualify for state courts of limited jurisdiction (such as small claims, justice of the peace, magistrate court, and similar courts with monetary limits of less than $30,000 on their jurisdictions over civil disputes), any controversy or claim, whether at law or equity, arising out of or related to the provision of services by UPS, regardless of the date of accrual of such dispute, shall be resolved in its entirety by individual (not class-wide nor collective) binding arbitration. Claimant and UPS expressly agree that the foregoing obliga- tion to arbitrate disputes regardless of the date of accrual of such disputes includes, but is not limited to, preexisting disputes and disputes that arise from or relate to Packages shipped at the time of a previous version of these Terms. Claimant and UPS further agree that the foregoing obliga- tion to arbitrate disputes applies to claims brought by UPS against Claimant, regard- less of whether Claimant has also brought a claim against UPS. Arbitration is the submission of a dispute to a neutral arbitrator, instead of a judge or jury, for a final and binding decision, known as an “award.” Arbitration provides for more limited discovery than in court, and is subject to limited review by courts. Each party has an opportunity to present evidence to the arbitrator in writing or through witnesses. An arbitrator can only award the same damages and relief that a court can award under the law and must honor the terms and conditions in the Terms.Claimant and UPS agree that their sole relationship is a contractual one governed by the Service Guide and Terms. Any controversy or claim arising out of or related to the provision of services by UPS shall be resolved solely based on the agreements set forth in the Service Guide and Terms. Project Technical Summary Document Operating Procedure (SOP) 1. Purpose/Scope The purpose of the Project Technical Summary document is to have a quick reference read on what the customers solution entails. This will help with scoping new features for the customer and give an overview of the solution to the integrators, SE, and support. 2. Responsibilities Who is responsible to write the Project Summary Technical Document and keeps it up to date. - When AST is involved in any project, the integrator is responsible to complete the Project Technical Summary Document (Per the requirements and example below) per project. Once completed the integrator will send it to the BA to review it and place it on SharePoint (TL - Shipping (Renna) > Customer Document Management Library > Customers > THE NAME OF THE CUSTOMER YOU ARE WORKING ON > Project Summary CUSTOMER NAME.doc) - When any vendor (Senecore, ConnectShip, Contax, ETC…) completes a project they will be required to complete a Project Technical Summary Document (Per the requirements and example below) and submit it to Chris Phillips CPhillips@ups.com. 3. Procedure/Process During the project closeout the PM will request the required Project Technical Summary Document. What is required in the Project Technical Summary Document Overview of the project - What are you integrating to o ERP name and version o How are you getting data ▪ Text ﬁle ▪ API ▪ Database ▪ Etc.… - Do they use any special services? o WorldEase o Trade Direct o Haz Mat - Any special coding done o Developed a custom service? o Etc.… - Custom documents o Custom Labels? o Custom Pick/Pack? o Any other custom documents or labels? - List the carriers o Any vars used ▪ Senecore The information contained in and accompanying this communication may be privileged or confidential and is intended solely for the use of the intended recipient(s). If you are not the intended recipient of this communication, please delete and destroy all copies immediately. Version X.X ▪ ConnectShip ▪ Locker ▪ Contax ▪ Etc.… - How many shippers - UPS Rates o Imported rate ﬁle o UPS Adapter rates - ShipExec o Using SBR? o Using CBR? o ShipExec Cloud? o ShipExec on-premises? ▪ ShipExec version ▪ Using the Client ▪ Using the API - ANY OTHER IMPORTANT INFORMATION TO KNOW ABOUT THE SOLUTION 4. References All Project Technical Summary Documents will be placed on SharePoint (TL - Shipping (Renna) > Customer Document Management Library > Customers > THE NAME OF THE CUSTOMER YOU ARE WORKING ON > Project Summary CUSTOMER NAME.doc) Example Location with Project Summary document: TL - Shipping (Renna) - THE STOW COMPANY - All Documents (sharepoint.com) 5. Appendix See the below Project Technical Summary Document example. 7. Text: UPS/CS Help Folder Locations C:\ProgramData\ConnectShip\Progistics\Events C:\Program Files (x86)\ConnectShip\Progistics\AMP\AMPService Logging Request/Response from PMC 1. Navigate to C:\Program Files (x86)\ConnectShip\Progistics\AMP\AMPService 2. Open the AMPService.exe.config file 3. Add the following above system.serviceModal <system.diagnostics> <sources> <source name="Progistics.MessageProcessor" switchValue="Verbose" messageTracing="true"> <listeners> <add name="textlistener"/> </listeners> </source> </sources> <sharedListeners> <add name="textlistener" type="System.Diagnostics.TextWriterTraceListener" initializeData="C:\ConnectShipLogs\AMPLog.txt" traceOutputOptions="DateTime,ThreadId" /> </sharedListeners> <trace autoflush="true" /> </system.diagnostics> 4. Restart AMP Service 5. Additionally (might not be necessary) - Open PMC 6. Under EventHandlers, there will be a list of services 7. For the ones that you need to log, set Plain Text File equal to true 8. Under Capture, check Debug for more logging 9. Restart AMP Service again

To do it right, rely on UPS From origin to destination, UPS can streamline your international shipping. As a worldwide leader in logistics, UPS’s tools, resources and integrated global net- work allow for more efficient process ing every day. Be confident that your shipments will arrive when they’re needed, wherever they’re needed around the world. This guide will help simplify your interna- tional shipping with information about: • International shipping forms — Why you need them, when you need them, and how to fill them out correctly • Technology solutions — Advanced systems that help make international shipping easier than ever before • International services — A review of the worldwide import and export services you can count on for guaranteed door-to-door delivery and customs clearance A world of shipping, simplified Your UPS® International How-to Guide
International shipping forms Governments worldwide require documentation with every international shipment to monitor and regulate the movement of goods across borders. Today’s businesses must navigate these regulations, as well as free trade agreements and varying duty rates. Accurate and complete documentation is critical to keep your shipments moving and avoid costly delays. UPS can help you determine which forms are required for your specific shipment. You can speed completion by filling out these forms online. And you can reduce data-entry errors by saving the finished forms in your history and reusing them for up to 90 days. Access our comprehensive library of international forms at ups.com. UPS International Customer Service Center 1-800-782-7892 ups.com/international 2
Definition and use of a document Before reviewing all of the supporting forms required for international shipping, there is an important factor to consider. When is it essential to include import or export documentation with a shipment, and when is it not required? It depends on what you’re shipping. Generally, international shipping forms are not required to ship documents, while they are required for shipping non-documents. What is a document? A document is generally defined as a written, typed or printed communication of no commercial value. Various countries define the term “document” differently. To view items considered docu- ments for your import country, please refer to the Country Regulations tool on ups.com. If you have questions, call the International Customer Service Center at 1-800-782-7892. UPS International Customer Service Center 1-800-782-7892 ups.com/international 3
Commercial invoice A guide to completing your export commercial invoice The commercial invoice (or pro forma invoice) is the customs document you’ll use most often when shipping outside the U.S. Filling out export documentation can seem daunting if you’re new to it. Incorrect information is the most common cause for customs delays, so getting it right matters. As one of the world’s largest customs brokers, UPS is known for fast, efficient customs clearance, which is why we’ve created this guide to help you avoid delays. It breaks down the form into easy-to-understand sections. The two main areas to watch out for are: • Description of goods • Goods valuation Always be accurate in your declaration. State as much as you know

customs clearance, which is why we’ve created this guide to help you avoid delays. It breaks down the form into easy-to-understand sections. The two main areas to watch out for are: • Description of goods • Goods valuation Always be accurate in your declaration. State as much as you know about the goods being exported. Make sure your documentation clearly communicates the reason for your export. Don’t worry if you don’t have some of the information at hand like a Harmonized Tariff code or Schedule B number. By choosing UPS, we can help you determine those entries. Explore this guide and uncover some tips from our top compliance experts. Our expert brokerage knowledge combined with UPS logistics will help you master exporting. Find out how to prepare each section of your invoice step-by-step. A FROM Please include full details, including Tax ID, contact name, address with postal code and country, and phone number (very important). B SHIPMENT DETAILS 1. Waybill number The waybill number is the tracking number shown on your shipping label (automatically generated when you create your shipment in UPS shipping systems). If there is more than one package, this should be the lead, or first, tracking number. 2. Shipment ID The shipment ID is the 11-digit version of the tracking number when using UPS technology to complete the invoice (i.e., 1YE595012345678901 becomes Shipment ID E5950123456). 3. Date Not necessarily the date that the ship- ment is handed to UPS. This should be the date the transaction took place in the seller’s records. (The date on the shipping label should show the date the shipment is handed to UPS.) TIP: Getting the date of sale/transaction correct is particularly important when goods are being purchased under a letter of credit. 4. Invoice number The invoice number is assigned by the shipper, if applicable. 5. Purchase order number Purchase order number is assigned by the shipper, if applicable. 6. The details of the description should include: • What it is • What materials it’s made of • What the item is used for • What the item’s serial or part number is, if applicable For example, an accurate description would be: ½ inch diameter carbide steel drill bit for machining metal, part 123-456. The detailed descriptions you provide are used by the desti­ nation brokerage department to assess and apply the import country’s duty and tax on the goods being sent. It’s important to recognize that all materials have an intrin- sic value, too. For example, even if you’re sending a product sample, your valuation should at a minimum represent the cost of the materials it took to produce it. Certificates of Origin U.S. Certificate of Origin (CO) • Some countries require a Certificate of Origin only for certain types of goods. To check if the country where you’re shipping requires a Certificate of Origin, please refer to the Country Regulations tool on ups.com or call the UPS Interna­ tional Customer Service Center at 1­800­782­7892. • You can use this form to authenticate that the country of origin of the merchandise being shipped is the United States. The form can only be used for goods that were pro­ duced or manufactured in the U.S. • Some nations restrict imports from specific countries. Many limit the quantity of goods that are allowed to be imported, or give preference to goods manufactured in certain countries. NAFTA Certificate of Origin • Determine whether your product qualifies for a reduction or elimination of duty under the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA). For information regarding eligi­ bility, contact your local U.S. Customs and Border Protection office. If it quali­ fies, the NAFTA Certificate of Origin form should be used to receive the ben­ efits of reduced duty. Generally, the importer benefits from the reduction in duty. • The certificate is required to qualify shipments between the U.S., Canada and Mexico for the reduced or eliminated duty allowed under NAFTA. • A shipment should be valued at greater than: – US$1,000 and be sent to a Mexican destina­ tion from Canada or the U.S. – CAD$2,500 and be sent to

is required to qualify shipments between the U.S., Canada and Mexico for the reduced or eliminated duty allowed under NAFTA. • A shipment should be valued at greater than: – US$1,000 and be sent to a Mexican destina­ tion from Canada or the U.S. – CAD$2,500 and be sent to a Canadian desti­ nation from Mexico or the U.S. – US$2,500 and be sent to a U.S. destination from Canada or Mexico. • If you ship the same commodity regularly, you can fill out one NAFTA form that provides coverage through one year. Complete the blanket period fields on the NAFTA form and give it to UPS. Once you execute a blanket NAFTA Certificate of Origin, the importer is then entitled to use the certificate to claim NAFTA tariff preference for all products speci­ fied on the certificate imported within the coverage period. There is no need to com­ plete a NAFTA Certificate of Origin for each subsequent shipment. U.S. Electronic Export Information (EEI), formerly known as Shipper’s Export Declaration (SED) • The EEI is data the U.S. government uses to develop export statistics and controls. It is required for shipping single commodities valued at more than $2,500 or commodi­ ties requiring a license or license exemption, regardless of value. • It is mandatory for export shipments originating in the U.S. and Puerto Rico to all destinations except Canada. Shipments to Canada only require an EEI if the ship­ ment contains licensed com­ modities, is tran­ siting Canada, or has a license exemption. • UPS online ship­ ping systems and the international forms application have simplified the Automated Export System (AES) process. Fill out the EEI online, then provide a paper copy to UPS. We can file it electronically, or you can access AESDirect through our online systems, file an EEI electronically with the Census Bureau, and give the AES transaction number to UPS (register at aesdirect.census.gov). NOTE: The U.S. government requires that all EEIs be filed electronically prior to export. UPS can file electronically on your behalf if we have a Power of Attorney on file for you. Incoterms ® A required field on the Commercial Invoice is the Terms of Sale, or Incoterms. Incoterms is an abbreviation for International Commercial Terms. They are a set of rules which define the responsibilities of sellers and buyers for the delivery of goods under sales contracts for domestic and international trade. The most commonly used terms for all modes of transportation are: • ExWorks (EXW): The seller fulfills his obligations by having the goods available for the buyer to pick up at his premises or another named place (i.e., factory, warehouse, etc.). Buyer bears all risks and costs starting when he picks up the products at the seller’s location until the products are delivered to his location. Seller has no obligation to load the goods or clear them for export. • Free Carrier (FCA): The seller delivers the goods export-cleared to the carrier stipulated by the buyer or another party authorized to pick up goods at the seller’s premises or another named place. Buyer assumes all risks and costs associated with delivery of goods to final destination including transportation after delivery to carrier and any customs fees to import the product into a foreign country. • Carriage Paid To (CPT): The seller clears the goods for export and delivers them to the carrier or another person stipulated by the seller at a named place of shipment. Seller is responsible for the transporta- tion costs associated with delivering goods to the named place of destination but is not responsible for procuring insurance. • Carriage and Insurance Paid To (CIP): The seller clears the goods for export and delivers them to the carrier or another person stipulated by the seller at a named place of shipment. Seller is responsible for the transportation costs associated with delivering goods and procuring minimum insurance coverage to the named place of destination. • Delivered at Terminal (DAT): The seller clears the goods for export and bears all risks and costs associated with delivering the goods and unloading them at the terminal at the named port or place of destina- tion. Buyer is responsible for all costs and risks from this point forward including clearing the goods for import at the named country of destination. • Delivered at Place (DAP): The seller clears the goods for export and bears all risks and costs associated with delivering the goods to the named place of destination not unloaded.

costs and risks from this point forward including clearing the goods for import at the named country of destination. • Delivered at Place (DAP): The seller clears the goods for export and bears all risks and costs associated with delivering the goods to the named place of destination not unloaded. Buyer is responsible for all costs and risks associated with unloading the goods and clearing customs to import the goods into the named country of destination. • Delivered Duty Paid (DDP): The seller bears all risks and costs associ- ated with delivering the goods to the named place of destination ready for unloading and cleared for import. WorldEase is a consolidated international shipping solution offered by UPS. It lets you combine multiple individual international shipments that are going to the same destination country into a single consolidated shipment. Think of it like this: Instead of sending 10 separate international packages from the U.S. to Canada, you put them all into one master shipment that clears customs as one unit—then once it’s in the destination country, UPS breaks it apart and delivers each individual package. ● Faster Customs Clearance: Only one customs entry needed. ● Lower Costs: You can save on customs brokerage and clearance fees. ● Simplified Paperwork: One set of customs documents for the master shipment. ● Efficient Tracking: Each individual shipment is still trackable, but moves as part of the master. What is the Entry Point or POE? It’s essentially the UPS hub or facility in the destination country that will: ● Receive the consolidated shipment ● Handle customs clearance ● Break it down for final delivery What is a POE File? POE stands for Port of Entry file. In the context of UPS WorldEase via ShipExec, the POE file is a manifest file that contains detailed information about all the individual shipments within a WorldEase master shipment. Think of it as a digital manifest that tells UPS and customs: ● What’s inside the consolidated shipment ● Who the recipients are ● What the declared values and contents are ● Where it’s all going The EU is a customs union, but WorldEase requires separate customs entries per country — so UPS needs to know if your consolidated shipment is: ● All for one EU country → use WORLD_EASE_SINGLE_EU_COUNTRY ● Split across multiple EU countries → invalid for standard WorldEase So, this flag enforces the rule that WorldEase shipments must be for a single destination country, even within the EU. If you try to create a WorldEase master with shipments going to Germany, France, and Italy under one consolidation, ShipExec or UPS will likely: ● Reject the POE file ● Fail customs clearance ● Trigger an error like: "Mixed destination countries not allowed in WorldEase Single EU Country mode" Use WORLD_EASE_SINGLE_EU_COUNTRY when: ● You’re shipping to one EU country (e.g., all packages going to Germany) ● You want to use WorldEase consolidation ● You want to leverage simplified customs clearance and cost savings When Do You Use WORLD_EASE_SINGLE_EU_COUNTRY? Scenario Use WORLD_EASE_SINGLE_EU_COUNT RY? All packages going to Germany Yes All packages going to France Yes Packages split across Germany and Austria No (must split shipments) Packages going to non-EU country (e.g. Canada, UK) No (use standard WORLD_EASE) World Ease World Ease consolidates several international shipments into a single shipment for customs clearance. 'World Ease Shipments' must be enabled under the Contract Options Tab (Permitted Special Service Options). ● World Ease POE data must be loaded for the shipper. To obtain your UPS POE data file, please contact your UPS representative. ● Set WORLD_EASE_FLAG to true, and provide an IMPORTER_OF_RECORD and IMPORTER_OF_RECORD_ACCOUNT. (This is mandatory) ● The valid services are EXPPLS, EXP, EXPSVR, EPD, STD, and NA1. ● Not supported with PACKAGING LETTER, BOX10KG or BOX25KG. ● WORLD_EASE_ID is used to save the group id for World Ease consolidated movements. ● World Ease labels and documents are listed here. Dynamic Bundling Shipment aggregation will occur for qualified packages for World Ease shipments as long as the option to 'Disable Automatic World Ease Child Shipment Aggregation' on the Advanced tab of Account Configuration is not enabled. See Dynamic Bundling for more information. World Ease Group Group Description OpenGroup To open a closed group. CloseGroup To close a open group. CreateGroup Not supported for World Ease Group. Group creation is determined and controlled by the component. ModifyGroup Not supported for World Ease Group. Group data cannot be modified after creating. Possible Group Status Open, Closed or Closed/Locked (This will happen if the manifest is closed without closing grous) World Ease Grouping Criteria The following table lists all fields that may be used as grouping criteria. The following information is used to consolidate packages into a group and must be the same for all packages in a group unless otherwise noted. Dictionary Term Description ADDITIONAL_HARDCOPY_DOCUMENTATIO N COMMENTS COMMERCIAL_INVOICE_METHOD CONSIGNEE_THIRD_PARTY_BILLING CONSIGNEE_THIRD_PARTY_BILLING_ACCO UNT When CONSIGNEE_THIRD_PARTY_ BILLING is true. CONSOLIDATION_TRACKING_NUMBER CUSTOMS_BROKER_CODE When SUBCATEGORY is STD and the origin is Canada and the destination is US DOCUMENTATION_CONSIGNEE Must be the same name address EXPORT_DECLARATION_STATEMENT EXPORT_REASON EXPORTER Must be the same name address. IMPORTER_OF_RECORD Must be the same name address. SHIPDATE SHIPPER TERMS TERMS_OF_SALE THIRD_PARTY_BILLING THIRD_PARTY_BILLING_ACCOUNT When THIRD_PARTY_BILLING is true. WORLD_EASE_CODE Note: ● Port of Entry (POE) must be same for one group. ● POE data is found based on service and destination. ● For EU destination, POE may also depend on WORLD_EASE_SINGLE_EU_ COUNTRY. ● Package count in one group cannot exceed 999 for US/PR shippers and 99,999 for all other shippers. Special Notes During closeout, if any packages in a World Ease group are "held", these packages will be assigned to a new group or an existing open group so that the "held" packages will not be included in End of Day PLD file. However, all WorldEase documents must be generated before the "held" packages are released to make sure previously "held" packages are not included in the documents.
